channel.title: Phoenix Technologies, Inc. — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Phoenix Technologies, Inc. (phoenix).

item.title: CVE-2024-12533 · 3.3 LOW
item.description: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in Phoenix SecureCore Technology 4 allows Input Data Manipulation.This issue affects SecureCore Technology 4: from 4.0.1.0 before 4.0.1.1018, from 4.1.0.1 before 4.1.0.573, from 4.2.0…

item.title: CVE-2024-29980 · 4.6 MEDIUM — Unsafe Handling of IHV UEFI Variables
item.description: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Kaby Lake, Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Coffee Lake, Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Comet Lake, Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Ice Lake allows Input Da…

item.title: CVE-2024-29979 · 4.6 MEDIUM — Unsafe Handling of Phoenix UEFI Variables
item.description: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions vulnerability in Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Kaby Lake, Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Coffee Lake, Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Comet Lake, Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Ice Lake allows Input Da…

item.title: CVE-2024-1598 · 7.5 HIGH — Potential buffer overflow when handling UEFI variables
item.description: Potential buffer overflow in unsafe UEFI variable handling in Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Gemini Lake.This issue affects: SecureCore™ for Intel Gemini Lake: from 4.1.0.1 before 4.1.0.567.

item.title: CVE-2024-0762 · 7.5 HIGH — Potential buffer overflow when handling UEFI variables
item.description: Potential buffer overflow in unsafe UEFI variable handling in Phoenix SecureCore™ for select Intel platforms This issue affects: Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Kaby Lake: from 4.0.1.1 before 4.0.1.998; Phoenix SecureCore™ for Intel Coffee Lake: from 4…

item.title: CVE-2023-35841 · 7.8 HIGH — WinFlash Driver Permissions Issue
item.description: Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control in Phoenix WinFlash Driver on Windows allows Privilege Escalation which allows for modification of system firmware.This issue affects WinFlash Driver: before 4.5.0.0.

item.title: CVE-2023-5058
item.description: Improper Input Validation in the processing of user-supplied splash screen during system boot in Phoenix SecureCore™ Technology™ 4 potentially allows denial-of-service attacks or arbitrary code execution.

item.title: CVE-2023-31100 · 8.4 HIGH
item.description: Improper Access Control in SMI handler vulnerability in Phoenix SecureCore™ Technology™ 4 allows SPI flash modification. This issue affects SecureCore™ Technology™ 4: * from 4.3.0.0 before 4.3.0.203 * from 4.3.1.0 before 4.3.1.163 * from 4.4.0.0 befo…
